items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution, privilege escalation, and security feature bypass. Adobe is not aware of any exploits in the wild for these issues.

items.title: Privilege Escalation via File Upload Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A critical file upload vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to privilege escalation. This was addressed as part of the APSB26-73 security update.

items.title: Arbitrary Code Execution via Improper Encoding in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: An improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to arbitrary code execution. This issue was resolved in the APSB26-73 security update.

items.title: Unauthenticated File Upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. Mirasvit released a patched version (1.11.12) on May 25, 2026.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This update addresses critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass. Adobe is not aware of any exploits in the wild.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: An incorrect authorization vulnerability in Adobe Commerce allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access. This flaw has a CVSS 3.1 base score of 7.5.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that enables unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files by disguising code as an image. Adobe released a fix in Magento 2.4.9.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This critical security update resolves multiple vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, execute malicious code, and compromise customer accounts. Adobe recommends applying patches quickly to avoid potential attacks.
